message:
Refactor OpenCode connector to use idiomatic Rust patterns

Modernize src/connectors/opencode.rs to leverage Rust Edition 2024 features
and more idiomatic Option handling patterns for cleaner, more readable code.

What changed:
- Replaced nested if-let chains with let-and pattern syntax (RFC 2497)
  * Before: nested if since_ts.is_some() { if let (Some(since), Some(ts)) = ... }
  * After: if let (Some(since), Some(ts)) = (since_ts, msg.created_at) && ts <= since
  * Improves readability by flattening control flow and making the guard
    condition explicit in a single expression
- Replaced map_or(false, |ts| ts > since) with is_some_and(|ts| ts > since)
  * Stabilized in Rust 1.70, is_some_and is more idiomatic and self-documenting
  * Used in two locations: message retention during initial filtering (line 174)
    and post-filter message retention during since_ts application (line 237)
  * Reduces cognitive load by directly expressing "is Some AND predicate holds"

Technical rationale:
- let-and patterns (edition 2024 feature) allow combining pattern matching with
  boolean guards in a single if expression, eliminating nesting
- is_some_and is a standard library method that combines the Option check and
  predicate application, avoiding the verbose map_or boilerplate
- Both changes reduce line count without sacrificing clarity or correctness
- No behavioral changes: same filtering logic, same timestamp comparisons

Code quality improvements:
- Eliminates 4 levels of nested if statements in the message filtering loop
- Makes the "skip messages older than since_ts" logic immediately apparent
- Uses more expressive standard library methods that communicate intent clearly
- Aligns with Rust community best practices for Option handling

This refactoring is purely stylistic and does not change any connector behavior,
indexing logic, or test expectations. All existing tests continue to pass.
